items.title: Moody’s notes Johor integrates into Singapore strategies, Malaysia emerges as fast‑growing data centre market
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — Malaysia, particularly Johor, has positioned itself as a cost-efficient alternative to Singapore for data centre investments, owing to its relatively abundant land, availability of power infrastructure, and attractive investment incentives. Additionally, Moody’s Ratings said Malaysia, one of the fastest-growing data centre markets in South and Southeast Asia, has benefited from available power and transmission infrastructure, supported by its solid power reserve margin, enabling rapid scaling of data centre capacity. “Failure to deliver on these power sector initiatives will likely constrain Malaysia’s ability to support further growth in data centre development, as power demand from data centres is already significant and increasing.

items.title: Penang to review RM1b Jelutong landfill project next week
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang Development Corporation (PDC) is expected to present its recommendation on the RM1 billion Jelutong landfill rehabilitation and reclamation project at the next state executive council meeting next week. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said PDC had yet to table the recommendation at last week's exco meeting as the agency required more time to prepare. Chow said the presentation is expected to be made at the next Exco meeting.

items.title: IJM Land, Southern Catalyst formalise JV for RM1.96bil industrial park in sedenak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: IJM Land Bhd has formalised a joint venture (JV) with Southern Catalyst Sdn Bhd to develop a RM1.96 billion industrial park in Sedenak, Johor, within the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ). In a joint statement today, IJM Land and Southern Catalyst said the development has an estimated gross development value of RM1.96 billion and is expected to be undertaken over six years to eight years. The parties said the partnership will be undertaken through a JV company, IJM Land Sedenak Sdn Bhd, in which IJM Land holds a 70 per cent equity stake while Southern Catalyst holds the remaining 30 per cent.

items.title: Sabah needs more child developmental specialists for autism cases, says expert
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah requires child developmental specialists to address autism cases, a medical expert has said. Sabah Malaysian Medical Council chairman Dr Brandon Patrick Senagang said the number of registered children diagnosed with autism has risen by over 600 per cent in a decade. In Sabah, 7,057 children have been diagnosed with autism, according to the Sabah Social Welfare Services Department's latest statistics.

items.title: SAR team expands search radius to 0.5 square kilometres for missing Hulu Selangor teen hiker
items.summary: HULU SELANGOR, May 25 — The search and rescue (SAR) operation for a 19-year-old girl believed to have gone missing during a hiking activity at Bukit Changkat Asa here entered its third day today, with the search radius expanded to 0.5 square kilometres. “Today’s operation also involves the use of drone units from the Selangor Government Disaster Management Unit,” she said. Azliza added that agencies involved in the operation included the Malaysia Defence Force (APM), the Forestry Department, the Department of Wildlife and National Parks Peninsular Malaysia (Perhilitan), and the Forestry Mountain Guide Association.

items.title: Batu Ferringhi losing appeal as coastal erosion worsens
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Severe coastal erosion in Batu Ferringhi is eroding its appeal among beachgoers, with visitors and tourism operators expressing concern about worsening conditions. A check over the weekend found the beach unusually quiet despite the school holiday, with fewer visitors seen swimming or picnicking along the once-popular stretch. The coastal erosion led to the collapse of several century-old casuarina trees earlier this year and is now threatening pedestrian walkways near the beachfront.

items.title: 'Happy birthday sister' WhatsApp message becomes offshore worker's final memory
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: A simple "Happy birthday sister, may you be blessed with rizq and ease in all matters" message sent in a family WhatsApp group has become the final memory of the late Nik Muhammad Hafifi Asri Ab Majid, 38. His youngest sister, Nurul Ain Shazwani Ab Majid, 30, said the message was the last they received from him before the tragedy. She said the family did not expect the seemingly ordinary birthday greeting to become his final memory.

items.title: Swiss divided as population cap vote nears
items.summary: THE Swiss will vote next month on an initiative to cap the population at 10 million – an anti-immigration proposal that is fracturing public opinion in the wealthy Alpine nation. "No to a Switzerland of 10 million inhabitants," is the name of the project launched by the country's biggest party, the hard-right Swiss People's Party (SVP), renowned for its opposition to immigration and closer ties with the European Union. The initiative's backers maintain that Switzerland, with its 9.1 million people, is buckling under the burden of "mass immigration", calling for a strict population cap.

items.title: Banks in Malaysia, APAC economies less impacted by higher energy prices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Banks in Malaysia and some Asia-Pacific economies will be less impacted by higher energy prices, according to Moody's Ratings. This is due to their economies' diversification and resilience, significant oil reserves, diversified energy mixes and strong government balance sheets, said the credit ratings agency in a report today. It said sustained high energy prices due to a prolonged Middle East conflict would impact Asia Pacific banks' credit profiles, via their loan portfolios and financial channels.

items.title: Zetrix AI, Shenzhen Data Exchange to jointly develop cross-border data trading platform
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Zetrix AI Bhd and Shenzhen Data Exchange Co Ltd plan to jointly establish a cross-border platform for data trading between Asean and China. The companies said the initiative seeks to unlock the value of data across Asean, where data remains largely underutilised despite its significant value creation potential. The collaboration will also promote the two-way circulation of cross-border data products and establish an Asean-China data interconnection framework.

items.title: Financial issues behind delay in Ayer Itam–LCE bypass project, says Chow
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Delays in the Ayer Itam–Tun Dr Lim Chong Eu bypass project are due to financial issues faced by the concessionaire, says Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow. However, he said the project had not exceeded its revised completion deadline of April 2027. The project was awarded to Consortium Zenith Construction Sdn Bhd (formerly Consortium Zenith-BUCG Sdn Bhd) in September 2013.

items.title: #NSTviral: Wheelie stunt ends in crash after delivery rider hits SUV
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A food delivery rider has gone viral after dashcam footage showed him attempting a wheelie stunt before crashing into an SUV. The footage drew swift backlash online, with netizens condemning the stunt as reckless and irresponsible. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: [UPDATED] RM4.4mil syabu seized in bold drug bust 100m from GOF post
items.summary: TANJUNG SEPAT: Two Indonesian men were caught in a bold attempt to smuggle out drugs just 100 metres from a General Operations Force (GOF) outpost. The suspects were detained in a raid carried out by Battalion 6 GOF at about 11.30am near a large pipeline in Jalan Ladang Tumbok Belangkan. Central Brigade GOF commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Hakemal Hawari said the suspects had picked the location near the GOF Ladang Tumbok outpost to wait for a boat believed to be arriving to transport the drugs out of the country.

items.title: Elderly man fights off crocodile, rides injured to relative's home for help
items.summary: The victim's daughter, Nursyuhadah Jamaludin, 25, said her father had gone out netting as part of his usual routine before the incident occurred. "Sometimes he went shrimp netting, sometimes he did gardening," she said. The crocodile immediately attacked him," she said.

items.title: Search underway for missing female hiker at Gunung Batu Putih
items.summary: Its director Datuk Sayani Saidon said the department received an emergency call at 11.32am yesterday regarding the disappearance of Jaslinda Saludin, 49. He said Jaslinda was among 14 participants and two Forestry Mountain Guides (MGPs) who began the Trans Spencer Chapman trek via the Pos Gedung–Gunung Bah Gading–Gunung Batu Putih–Kuala Woh route at 2am on May 23. Sayani said Jaslinda was with another hiker, Mohd Hanafi Neikmad, 41, who experienced health complications during the climb.

items.title: Second teenager’s body found floating near Dungun jetty after Sungai Beril drowning tragedy
items.summary: DUNGUN, May 25 — The body of the second teenager who drowned in Sungai Beril near Taman Beril, Paka, on Sunday was found this morning. Dungun acting district police chief DSP Jumaidy Bah Chong Weh said members of the public spotted the body of Muhammad Aidil Amsyar Mohd Zul-Zunaidi, 14, floating near the Paka Market jetty at 9.55am. He said the body of the Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Agama Durian Guling student was located about two kilometres from where the incident occurred.

items.title: Go after syndicate bosses, not runners, say MCA, MCPF
items.summary: MCA Public Services and Complaints Department head Datuk Seri Michael Chong said innocent homeowners and families were increasingly being targeted despite having no connection to the debts involved. He said syndicates often hire runners online to splash paint or carry out Molotov cocktail attacks on homes for RM400 to RM800. This is murder," he said.

items.title: B15 implementation in Peninsular Malaysia will not affect palm oil exports -- Noraini
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The B15 biodiesel implementation in Peninsular Malaysia starting June 1 is set to strengthen the country's energy security without affecting palm oil export volume while also helping to support the local palm oil industry and accelerate the national green agenda. "We produce about 20 million metric tonnes of palm oil annually and export around 16 million metric tonnes. Noraini said the B15 implementation is also expected to help boost demand for local palm oil while reducing the country's dependence on imported fossil-based diesel.

items.title: Anwar pledges targeted Budi Madani support for smallholders, entrepreneurs, vulnerable groups
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — The government has agreed to continue the Budi Madani: Budi Agri-Komoditi initiative in a targeted manner to assist smallholders, entrepreneurs and vulnerable groups who are the backbone of the country’s economy. “Based on these considerations, the government has agreed to continue the Budi Madani: Budi Agri-Komoditi initiative in a targeted manner to assist smallholders, entrepreneurs and vulnerable groups who are the backbone of the country’s economy,” he said in a Facebook post today. Regarding the people’s health security, Anwar said that overall, the supply of medicines in the country is still stable and MOH will continue to closely monitor any challenges involving the stock chain and prices to prevent the people from becoming victims of the situation.

items.title: Rubio: Iran deal could materialise 'today'
items.summary: NEW DELHI: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Monday that a deal to end the war with Iran could materialise "today", adding that Israel had the right to defend itself against attack. "We thought we might have some news last night, maybe today, I wouldn't read too much into it," Rubio said in New Delhi, referring to the potential agreement. Rubio told reporters that "Israel always has a right to protect itself."

items.title: Online job market records 587,801 vacancies in Q4 2025 - DOSM
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: A total of 587,801 job vacancies were advertised online in the fourth quarter of 2025, according to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) in its publication Big Data Analytics: Job Market Insights – Online Job Vacancies in Malaysia, Q4 2025. Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said the number of companies advertising jobs rose to 80,287 in the fourth quarter from 77,615 in the previous quarter. Mohd Uzir added that the services sector remained the largest contributor, accounting for 84.4 per cent or 496,065 online job advertisements in the fourth quarter, compared with 77.2 per cent in the previous quarter.

items.title: DOSM says 587,801 job vacancies advertised online in Q4 2025, services sector leads
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 25 — A total of 587,801 job vacancies were advertised online in the fourth quarter of 2025, according to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) in its publication Big Data Analytics: Job Market Insights – Online Job Vacancies in Malaysia, Q4 2025 . Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said the number of companies advertising jobs rose to 80,287 in the fourth quarter from 77,615 in the previous quarter. Mohd Uzir added that the services sector remained the largest contributor, accounting for 84.4 per cent or 496,065 online job advertisements in the fourth quarter, compared with 77.2 per cent in the previous quarter.

items.title: ISP Group secures GreenRE certification across select properties
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: ISP Group has secured GreenRE certification across a range of its industrial and workers' accommodation properties, positioning itself among a limited number of Malaysian industrial developers with verified green credentials spanning both asset classes. Its latest milestone includes ISP Astute warehouse in Shah Alam, which received GreenRE Silver Certification under the existing industrial facilities category. Meanwhile, ISP Home Kapar achieved the highest recognition to date with GreenRE gold certification.

items.title: Suhakam urges action against Taiping Prison officers involved in violent incident
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) has demanded immediate disciplinary action against Taiping Prison officers involved in last year's violent incident, strongly rejecting claims that doing so would trigger "double jeopardy". He said disciplinary action was warranted given the clear surveillance footage evidence demonstrating the misconduct. "The Public Inquiry Panel found that the Prisons Department failed to take any disciplinary action against the Taiping Prison officers involved, even though the Board of Inquiry found that standard operating procedures had been violated by personnel at Taiping Prison.

items.title: Online job market records 587,801 vacancies in Q4 2025: DOSM
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: A total of 587,801 job vacancies were advertised online in the fourth quarter of 2025, according to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) in its publication Big Data Analytics: Job Market Insights – Online Job Vacancies in Malaysia, Q4 2025. Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said the number of companies advertising jobs rose to 80,287 in the fourth quarter from 77,615 in the previous quarter. Mohd Uzir added that the services sector remained the largest contributor, accounting for 84.4 per cent or 496,065 online job advertisements in the fourth quarter, compared with 77.2 per cent in the previous quarter.

items.title: Pasir Mas ustaz faces four charges of sexually assaulting boys, producing obscene images
items.summary: PASIR MAS, May 25 — An ustaz, or a religious teacher, pleaded not guilty in the Magistrate’s Court here today to four charges of sexually assaulting two boys and producing obscene images. Muhammad Faiz Muhamad Ramly, 31, entered the plea before Magistrate Syed Farid Syed Ali. For the third and fourth charges, he was accused of committing physical sexual assault on a six-year-old boy and producing obscene images involving the child at a house in Kampung Layat, Jeli, in May 2025.

items.title: MISC posts higher Q1 net profit of RM741.4mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MISC Bhd reported a higher net profit of RM741.4 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26), compared with RM705.7 million in the corresponding quarter last year. The energy shipping company said revenue rose to RM2.89 billion, up RM75.3 million, or 2.7 per cent, from RM2.81 billion a year ago. MISC attributed its better performance to higher revenue from the petroleum and product shipping segment, primarily driven by higher freight rates and earning days, as well as higher revenue from the marine and heavy engineering segment, mainly due to ongoing projects advancing into higher construction phases, partially offset by lower revenue from post-sail-away projects.

items.title: Dread and denial at heart of deadly DR Congo Ebola outbreak
items.summary: UNLIKE other residents of Mongbwalu, a town at the heart of the eastern Democratic Republic of Congo's latest devastating Ebola outbreak, Laureine Sakiya believes that the blood-letting virus exists after seeing some of her neighbours die. Already suspicious of the Congolese state following decades of neglect and conflict, many in the outbreak's epicentre in the northeastern Ituri province are split between criticism of the government's response and denial of the disease's very existence. Of the 322 people suspected to have contracted Ebola in Mongbwalu – where many of the outbreak's first cases were recorded – 88 have died, according to the latest toll from the authorities.

items.title: Malaysia deepens defence ties with Türkiye as LMS Batch 2 programme launched in Istanbul
items.summary: ISTANBUL, May 25 — Malaysia has strengthened its defence-industrial cooperation with Türkiye through a series of engagements during the defence minister’s official working visit, which culminated in the naming and launching of the Royal Malaysian Navy’s Littoral Mission Ship (LMS) Batch 2. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the visit reflects growing trust and strategic cooperation between both countries, particularly in defence technology and industrial development. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin during a meeting with Desan in Turkiye.

items.title: Taiping Prison more fit as a museum, Suhakam says after inmate's death
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) has proposed that Taiping Prison cease operating as a detention facility and be converted into a museum following the discovery of multiple infrastructure and safety issues. Suhakam chairman Datuk Seri Mohd Hishamudin Md Yunus said the panel also proposed that the government build a new prison at a different location to replace the heritage-listed facility. "The Public Inquiry Panel recommends that the continued use of Taiping Prison at its current site be reviewed, taking into account its heritage status, infrastructure condition, building safety, as well as the welfare of inmates and prison personnel.

items.title: Pilgrims kick off haj as war's trajectory hangs in the balance
items.summary: MAKKAH: Over 1.5 million Muslims began filling a vast tent city in the holy city of Makkah on Monday for an annual hajj pilgrimage carried out against the backdrop of hopes for an end to the war in the Middle East. The white-robed pilgrims on buses or on foot arrived at the sprawling encampment in Mina after performing the "tawaf" – walking seven times around the Kaaba, the giant black cube at Mecca's Grand Mosque. Despite the uncertainty triggered by the conflict, Saudi officials noted over the weekend that more pilgrims had travelled from abroad to participate in this year's haj than in 2025.

items.title: Over 200,000 Bersatu members joining Hamzah's 'Reset' team, claims ex-Melaka CM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Melaka Bersatu chairman Tan Sri Abdul Rahim Thamby Chik has claimed that more than 200,000 party members have either left or are expected to leave the party to join the "Reset" team led by Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin. The former Melaka chief minister also rejected claims that Hamzah, the former Bersatu deputy president and opposition leader, was responsible for splitting the party. "When a political party begins to operate like a private limited company, selectively enforcing disciplinary action against members, allowing defeated leaders to be reinstated, tolerating leaders with questionable moral records, treating corruption lightly, abandoning the people's struggle, excessively glorifying leaders and branding criticism of the party as betrayal of its leadership, then naturally members will grow resentful of such practices and culture.

items.title: Malaysia's leading index rises 0.5pct in March
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's leading index climbed 0.5 per cent year-on-year in March to 113.3 points from 112.7 points last year, according to the Statistics Department (DOSM). "On the other hand, real import of semiconductors recorded a decline of 22.6 per cent, in line with more moderate external demand, thereby limiting the overall growth of the index," he said. DOSM said that the long-term smoothed trend of the leading index in March remained below 100.0 points reflecting a moderate growth momentum despite global uncertainty risks, including persistent inflationary pressures.

items.title: Excessive force, rights breaches found in Taiping Prison incident, says Suhakam
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) has found that Prisons Department personnel committed constitutional and human rights violations during a transfer operation at Taiping Prison. "The public inquiry panel found that excessive physical force was used by a large number of prison personnel against the high court detainees (TMT) during the transfer operation from Hall B to Block E on Jan 17, 2025. This violence also affected the group of detainees housed in Block B," Hishamudin said.

items.title: Malaysia Smelting Corp climbs to record on fivefold profit surge
items.summary: Malaysia Smelting Corp's net profit for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q2 FY26), leaped to RM42.93 million from RM7.72 million in the same quarter last year amid firmer tin prices and improved margins. The group said the tin smelting segment recorded a pre-tax profit of RM15.5 million in the quarter under review compared with RM5.7 million in Q1 FY25. It said the better performance was mainly due to higher profit from encashment of tin intermediates, contributed by a higher tin price and higher margin, despite lower tin encashment quantity.

items.title: Pahang MB conveys condolences to families of offshore platform tragedy victims
items.summary: KUANTAN: Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail has conveyed his condolences to the families of three contractors who were killed after their lifeboat plunged into the sea off Terengganu-Kelantan waters yesterday. The 12.50pm incident at the Sepat oil platform claimed the lives of Ahmad Fiqri Zakaria, 38, from Melaka; Muhammad Faezuan Hakim Mohammad Bustamam, 28; and Nik Muhammad Hafifi Asri Ab Majid, 37, both from Kuantan. The remains of Faezuan Hakim and Nik Muhammad Hafifi will be brought to Kuantan later today for burial at the Setali Muslim Cemetery.

items.title: Govt will continue to work hard, ensure the people are looked after, says PM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said the National Economic Action Council (NEAC) meeting focused on efforts to address mounting pressures stemming from the global supply chain crisis, particularly involving the healthcare and commodities sectors. In a post on his social media platforms, Anwar said the meeting heard key presentations from the Health Ministry and the Plantation and Commodities Ministry. He said the Madani government understood the plight of the people facing rising living costs and global economic uncertainties.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh: Food courts, hawker stalls and markets in Putrajaya to see rental rates reduced
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said the initiative, expected to benefit 244 petty traders in Putrajaya, was introduced to ease operational burdens amid the still challenging economic climate. Hannah said traders wishing to qualify for the rental reduction must first settle any outstanding rental arrears up to May 2026. “As the people continue facing various daily challenges, targeted assistance is essential to ensure petty traders can continue to survive,” she said.

items.title: ‘Purchasing power is in your hands’: Economy minister urges Malaysians to compare prices amid rising cost concerns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — Putrajaya today urged Malaysians to compare prices and make informed purchasing decisions amid the ongoing global supply pressures linked to the West Asia conflict. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the government is continuing weekly monitoring of domestic food prices while encouraging the public to use the government-run PriceCatcher mobile application to check and compare prices before making purchases. Compare prices.

items.title: Eleven men charged with alleged ‘RT6 Gang’ membership in KL Sessions Court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — A total of 11 men were charged in the Sessions Court here today with being members of an organised crime group known as the “RT6 Gang” since 2020. They are A. Raja Ratanam, K. Sunthreson, C. Vijaya Kumar, N. Nadarajah, T. Raman, R. Vijayan, M. Kumaaran, N. Harichandran, S. Maran, Suwaran Singh Minder Singh and Mohammad Atif Khan Mohammad Ali Khan, aged between 22 and 48. The prosecution was conducted by Deputy Public Prosecutors Lokman Kasim and Yazid Mustaqim Roslan, while the defence team comprised lawyers Datuk Rajpal Singh, Eu Kah Mun, M. Mathaven, Grace Francis, Muhammad Izzhar Idris, S. Kirthen, S. Premila and Kelvin Francis.

items.title: Sepat FSO tragedy: Police conduct detailed investigation into deaths of three workers
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, May 25 — Police are conducting a detailed investigation into the accident that claimed the lives of three workers at the Sepat floating storage and offloading (FSO) facility, located about 150 metres off the waters of Kuala Terengganu, yesterday afternoon. Kuala Terengganu police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said a team of officers from the Criminal Investigation Department had been dispatched to the location to determine the cause of the incident. — Bernama

items.title: Malaysia’s oil supply remains stable until July, Petronas assures amid global energy tensions, says minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — Petronas has assured Putrajaya that Malaysia’s national oil supply stock is sufficient until July 2026 despite ongoing instability in West Asia and continued tensions around the Strait of Hormuz, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said today. Akmal said the average Brent crude oil price between May 18 and 22 stood at US$111.67 (RM441.74) per barrel, up 1.7 per cent from US$109.85 the previous week. Coal prices also edged up marginally by 0.7 per cent to US$132.29 per metric tonne.

items.title: Man accused of stabbing woman in the neck in Kedah car attack to be charged tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — A man suspected of stabbing his female friend in the neck inside a car in Titi Gajah, Kepala Batas in Alor Setar, Kedash, on May 17 will be charged tomorrow. Kota Setar district police chief Assistant Commissioner Syed Basri Syed Ali confirmed the matter, saying the suspect will be charged at the Alor Setar Sessions Court, Harian Metro reported today. The suspect fled the scene but was arrested by police four hours later.

items.title: Kedah mechanic charged with three firearms offences in Sik Sessions Court, pleads not guilty
items.summary: SIK, May 25 — A mechanic was once again charged at the Sessions Court here today on three counts involving the possession of firearms, and pleaded not guilty to all charges. Mohd Shahifuldin Mohd Shahiran, 46, entered the plea before Judge Rohaida Ishak. On the third count, Mohd Shahifuldin was charged with possessing an imitation Glock 19 Gen 4 pistol and one bullet cartridge without lawful reason at the same place, time and date.

items.title: Malaysia’s medicine supply stable and sufficient despite Iran conflict, says economy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — Malaysia’s supply of medicines and medical devices remains stable and sufficient despite ongoing global supply disruptions linked to the Iran conflict, with only 16.8 per cent of medicine items currently classified as high risk, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said today. “From the overall recorded medicine items, 72 per cent are at low risk, 11.3 per cent at medium risk, and 16.8 per cent at high risk requiring continuous monitoring and mitigation measures,” he said. For medical devices, he said 81.7 per cent were categorised as low risk, 13.5 per cent as medium risk, while only 4.8 per cent were considered high risk.

items.title: GSF 2.0 activists return to KLIA after Israeli detention; one stable after lung surgery in Istanbul following assault injury
items.summary: SEPANG, May 25 — A total of 28 Malaysians who were detained by the Israeli army in the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 humanitarian mission to Gaza arrived home safely last night. All 28 volunteers involved were Norazman Ishak; Muhammad Hareez Adzrami; Nor Liyana Farhan Daud; Zainal Rashid Ahmad; Ahmad Musa Al-Nuwayri Kamaruzaman; Hazwan Hazim Dermawan; Norhelmi Ab Ghani; Mohd Hanafi Mohd Salim; and Mohd Shamsir Mohd Isa. Also joining the mission were Mohd Redzal Amzah; Muhammad Zaid Abdul Karim; Al-Ahmad Shah Abdul Kadir; Mohammad Farizzal Rostam; Chin Shiau Weai; Abdul Samad Selamat; Mohammad Shazly Mohammad Shees; Tengku Ahmad Taufik Tengku Noor Zahari; Syed Aminul Syafiq Syed Iskandar and Osman Zulkifli.

items.title: Three senators sworn in at Dewan Negara, including UniMAIWP vice-chancellor and two reappointments
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — Three senators, including a new appointee, took the oath of office before Senate President Datuk Awang Bemee Awang Ali Basah today. In his speech, Awang Bemee said Noor Inayah, who was elected by the Penang State Legislative Assembly, would serve a three-year term from May 14, 2026, until May 13, 2029. He said Lingeshwaran would also serve for the same period, while Hussin’s reappointment, which took effect on April 23, will end on April 22, 2029.

items.title: Malaysian Haj pilgrim dies of brain haemorrhage in Mecca, 10th fatality this season
items.summary: MECCA, May 25 — A 52-year-old female Haj pilgrim from Parit Jawa, Johor, became the 10th Malaysian pilgrim to die in the Holy Land during this year’s haj season after suffering a brain haemorrhage yesterday. Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH), in a statement, said the deceased, who had travelled alone to perform the haj, was a pilgrim from the KT08 group and was staying at the Rehab Al-Janadriyah Hotel. “She was pronounced dead at Shefaa Hospital at 5am due to a brain haemorrhage.

items.title: Man injured after being bitten by crocodile while fishing in Port Dickson
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — A man suffered injuries to his thigh and legs after being bitten by a crocodile in an incident at the Sungai Linggi riverbank in Kampung Tanjung Agas early this morning. In the 5am incident, the victim, Jaafa Hasan, 62, who is the secretary of the village development and security committee (JPKK), sustained serious injuries to both legs and his thigh, according to Kosmo! According to a local fisherman, the incident was shared in a fishermen’s WhatsApp group and went viral earlier today.

items.title: JPJ to restore online vehicle ownership transfer service immediately, Anthony Loke announces
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 25 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) has agreed to immediately restore its online transfer of vehicle ownership (THM) transaction service, with any future suspension for system upgrades to be carried out only during the long holiday period this weekend. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the decision was made following discussions with JPJ director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli, after media reports emerged last night about the suspension of the service. Therefore, JPJ needs to carry out improvements to the online system as soon as possible.

items.title: Ministry assures livestock supply surpasses demand for sacrificial rituals during Aidiladha
items.summary: Data from the Department of Veterinary Services (DVS) as of April showed that local cattle and buffalo stock stood at 35,127 heads, while imported supply totalled 16,845 heads. “This brings the overall supply of cattle and buffalo for sacrificial purposes to 51,972 heads, surpassing the estimated requirement of 40,420 heads for mosques, surau and slaughterhouses nationwide,” the ministry said. For goats and sheep, KPKM said local stock amounted to 25,815 heads, with imported supply recorded at 4,232 heads, bringing the total to 30,047 heads compared with the projected demand of 22,424 heads.

items.title: Petronas says investigations ongoing after fatal lifeboat incident at Sepat platform in Terengganu-Kelantan waters
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, May 25 — Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) has confirmed that three contractor personnel were killed while another was critically injured during lifeboat maintenance work at the Sepat platform in Terengganu-Kelantan waters yesterday. In a statement today, Petronas said investigations into the cause of the incident are ongoing in coordination with the relevant authorities. Last night Kuala Terengganu police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said the incident occurred at about 12.50pm when all four victims were believed to have boarded a lifeboat on the platform to descend to the sea for maintenance work on the lower section of the platform.

items.title: Child dies, 11 injured in three‑car collision near Muadzam Shah, Pahang JBPM confirms
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 25 — A boy was killed while 11 others were injured in an accident involving three vehicles on the Segamat-Kuantan Highway near Muadzam Shah early this morning. A spokesperson for the Pahang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) said the department received an emergency call at 1.12am before a team from the Muadzam Shah Fire and Rescue Station rushed to the scene. The spokesperson said the accident involved three vehicles — a Proton Saga, Perodua Alza, and Perodua Axia — each carrying four individuals.

items.title: Teenager among two killed in Kuala Kubu Bharu collision, woman sustains injuries
items.summary: Selangor Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant director (Operations) Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said six personnel from the Bukit Sentosa Fire and Rescue Station were dispatched to the scene after an emergency call was received at 12.14am. He said the crash involved a Proton Waja and a motorcycle. Ahmad Mukhlis said the 51-year-old driver sustained injuries.

items.title: Kajang SILK Highway crash: Couple seriously injured after speeding car loses control on downhill stretch
items.summary: According to Harian Metro , the 9.10pm incident left the two local victims, aged 60 and 61, with serious injuries to the head and arms. Kajang district police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said initial investigations found that the vehicle involved was travelling from Kampung Baru Balakong towards Kajang. “In the accident, a 35-year-old local male driver also sustained serious head injuries,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: MAF pledges compliance with Tengku Hassanal’s decree on military activities in Pahang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — The Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) has expressed its gratitude and pledged compliance with the decree issued by the Tengku Mahkota of Pahang, Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, regarding the conduct of military activities in the state. It said the MAF fully adheres to all regulatory guidelines, standing operating procedures (SOPs), and environmental impact assessments stipulated by the relevant authorities. “The MAF also recognises the importance of strategic cooperation with the state government, enforcement agencies, relevant experts, and local communities in every training deployment.

items.title: Two factories and workers’ hostel destroyed in Juru blaze, no casualties reported
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, May 25 — Two factories and a workers’ hostel were destroyed in a fire at Lorong Industri Ringan 4, Juru Light Industrial Area, here last night. Penang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant director (Operations) John Sagun Francis said firefighters rushed to the scene after receiving an emergency call at 8.25pm, adding that no casualties were reported in the incident. “A workers’ hostel measuring about 55.74 square metres was also destroyed in the fire,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Khaled confirms three new Littoral Mission Ships to boost Royal Malaysian Navy readiness
items.summary: ISTANBUL, May 25 — Malaysia is on track to receive three Littoral Mission Ship Batch 2 (LMSB2) being built in Turkiye by the end of next year, boosting the Royal Malaysian Navy’s (RMN) operational readiness in safeguarding national maritime sovereignty. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said construction has progressed on schedule since the contract was signed in June 2024. The first LMSB2, named Tunku Laksamana Abdul Jalil, was officially named and launched by Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia.

items.title: Anwar expected to officiate state-level Kaamatan Festival in Penampang on May 30
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 25 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is expected to officiate the state-level Kaamatan Festival celebration on May 30 at the Hongkod Koisaan Hall (KDCA) in Penampang. He said the closing ceremony on May 31 would also be graced by Sabah Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Musa Aman. On traffic and parking concerns during the celebration, Dr Jeffrey said the committee would provide free shuttle bus services for visitors.

items.title: Malaysia ready to take Israel to ICJ over alleged Gaza flotilla abuses, says Selangor MB
items.summary: Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the legal action follows alleged acts of brutality, including kidnapping and torture involving activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 humanitarian mission, particularly Malaysian participants. According to Utusan Malaysia , Amirudin said the move will be followed by continued diplomatic pressure by the government to demand the full liberation of Gaza. “We will bring this to the international court, we will continue diplomatic pressure, and we will also travel across Malaysia,” he said during the Global Sumud Flotilla 2.0 Homecoming Ceremony at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 1 on Monday night.
